affected_groups: Roma and other ethnic minority communities in affected settlements
Local governments and school maintainers negotiating with central authorities
Independent education-policy researchers relying on the data infrastructure
content.decision_question: Transparency and evidentiary equity versus the risk that formally neutral, advisory infrastructure becomes an instrument of centralized control that launders predetermined closures as evidence-based
content.evidence_boundary: Contested and currently unknown: no technical specification for the registry has been published yet, so whether it will in fact mandate settlement-size, ethnicity and income disaggregation, and whether procedural safeguards against selective use will be enforceable, cannot be verified; S5's own political-risk analysis already flags selective citation as a plausible failure mode, which is itself an admission of unresolved uncertainty rather than a settled fact
content.tension: A national transparency framework (thresholds plus a closure/outcome registry) is a genuine public good only if it publishes disaggregated data by settlement size, ethnicity and income and remains genuinely advisory rather than becoming a tool to justify predetermined closures.
content.title: Decision tension A20: S5
value_poles: the risk that formally neutral, advisory infrastructure becomes an instrument of centralized control that launders predetermined closures as evidence-based
